title: cyanea glabra
intro: Cyanea glabra, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or white hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant endemic to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have several health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood pressure. Cyanea glabra is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
